name: Erik Olin Wright
background: Real utopias theory; economic alternatives; cooperative economics; class analysis; authored 'Envisioning Real Utopias' (2010) on social empowerment and alternatives to capitalism
recruiting_angle: Ostrom-meets-Wright synthesis for Zone 5 cooperative economics; his 'real utopias' framework directly maps to viable local currency and cooperative design chapters

name: Jessica Gordon Nembhard
background: Cooperative economics; African American cooperative history; 'Collective Courage' (2014) on Black cooperative movement; solidarity economics; democratic economic institutions
recruiting_angle: Her cooperative case study methodology is exactly the empirical grounding the Domain A WIR and timebanking sections need; Midwest agricultural cooperative history has direct parallels to her documented cases

name: Stephanie Kelton
background: MMT and monetary theory; 'The Deficit Myth' (2020); community currency theory implications; sovereign currency alternatives; understands when and why local currencies are viable
recruiting_angle: Her framework for understanding money creation maps directly to how community currencies work at the micro level — she could anchor the 'what replaces dollars' sections with the clearest theoretical grounding of any candidate
compensation_option_recommended: Option 1 ($4000-5000) — higher rate given public profile

name: David Bollier
institution_organization: Schumacher Center for a New Economics / Reinventing the Commons
background: Commons governance; digital commons; 'Think Like a Commoner' (2014); P2P Foundation board; practical commons design for communities; extensive writing for non-academic audiences
recruiting_angle: His translation work — making Ostrom's commons theory accessible to practitioners — is the exact writing mode Domain A needs; he already understands the Zone 5 agricultural cooperative context through Schumacher Center work
compensation_option_recommended: Option 1 ($3000-4000) — he writes for broad audiences frequently

name: Marjorie Kelly
background: Democratic ownership; 'Owning Our Future' (2012); cooperative enterprise design; local economy theory; worker ownership transitions; has worked with Midwest cooperatives
recruiting_angle: Her work on 'generative ownership' and cooperative design for community resilience is the most practically-oriented academic framework available for Domain A cooperative enterprise chapters

name: John McNamara
institution_organization: US Federation of Worker Cooperatives (former Executive Director)
background: 25+ years in worker cooperative movement; cooperative governance; scaling cooperative networks; Zone 5 worker cooperative development; USFWC policy experience
recruiting_angle: Has directly managed the institutional questions Domain A addresses — how cooperatives allocate labor
priority_tier: and scale — from the inside of the movement rather than from academia

name: Rink Dickinson
background: 30+ years at premier US fair trade worker cooperative; democratic governance at scale; international cooperative sourcing; 'Can cooperatives survive the market?' practitioner knowledge; authored organizational analyses
recruiting_angle: Equal Exchange's 30-year track record is one of the strongest case studies available for how cooperatives sustain economic structures through market disruption — exactly the resilience frame Domain A needs

name: Cynthia Pansing
background: 25+ years in CDFI and cooperative finance in the Great Plains; agricultural cooperative capital formation; Zone 5 direct experience; rural credit union governance
recruiting_angle: Her geographic and sector expertise is directly Zone 5 — Nebraska agricultural finance
priority_tier: and rural economic resilience are her operational domain

name: Erbin Crowell
institution_organization: National Federation of Community Development Credit Unions / Neighboring Food Cooperative Association
background: Executive director of cooperative association; cooperative development education; food cooperative governance; 25+ years cooperative network building in Northeast with applicability to Midwest
recruiting_angle: His multi-cooperative federation experience maps directly to Domain A's 'inter-community trade and cooperative federation' sections — how 5-10 cooperatives coordinate their economic activity

name: Ann Hoyt
institution_organization: University of Wisconsin Cooperative Development / National Cooperative Business Association (retired)
background: 30+ years cooperative development education; trained hundreds of cooperative developers; USDA cooperative technical assistance; Zone 5 agricultural cooperative expertise
recruiting_angle: One of the most experienced cooperative development educators in the US — her curriculum development experience means she can structure Domain A's implementation templates in a format practitioners can actually use
compensation_option_recommended: Option 1 ($3000-4000) — curriculum expertise is high value

name: Mira Luna
background: Mutual aid network organizing; community currency and timebanking practitioner; 'Building Community Wealth' research contributor; community economics journalism for non-specialist audiences
recruiting_angle: Has been documenting and practicing community economic alternatives for 15+ years; her writing is already at the practitioner voice level Domain A needs — one of few candidates where the writing voice concern is pre-answered

name: Alnoor Ladha
institution_organization: Culture Hack Labs / The Next Economy
background: Post-capitalist economics; solidarity economy organizing; 'Post-Capitalist Politics' contributor; mutual aid theory and practice; gift economy design; global community currency networks
recruiting_angle: His gift economy and solidarity economics practitioner knowledge fills the 'what replaces wages' section with lived experience that no academic source library can provide

name: Nora Leccese
institution_organization: Solidarity Economy NYC / New York City Community Land Initiative
background: Community land trust organizing; mutual aid network building; solidarity economy mapping; cooperative housing; local economic system design for urban-to-rural transitions
recruiting_angle: Her community land trust work addresses Domain A's 'commons governance for shared resources' sections with direct operational experience; CLT governance is Ostrom's design principles in practice

name: Enric Duran
background: 10+ years building alternative economic systems; complementary currency design; mutual credit system implementation; community economic federation design; European community currency practitioner network
recruiting_angle: Has actually built and run mutual credit systems at community scale — the WIR parallel case study sections of Domain A require someone who understands these systems from the inside

name: Autumn Rowan
institution_organization: Mutual Aid Hub / COVID Mutual Aid Network Coordinator (Midwest)
background: Led Midwest COVID mutual aid networks 2020-2022; community resource allocation without market prices; skills exchange organizing; labor contribution tracking systems; Zone 5 direct experience
recruiting_angle: Her 2020-2022 mutual aid network experience is the most directly relevant contemporary case study for Domain A's 'allocation without prices' and 'labor contribution tracking' sections

name: Sam Bliss
institution_organization: University of Vermont Gund Institute for Environment
background: Degrowth economics; post-growth community economics; 'A Degrowth Transition Primer'; community sufficiency economics; has written for both academic and practitioner audiences
recruiting_angle: His degrowth economics framework provides the theoretical scaffolding for why communities need alternative economic systems — and his practitioner-facing writing is already at the voice level Domain A needs

name: Janelle Cornwell
institution_organization: UMass Amherst Labor Center / US Solidarity Economy Network
background: Solidarity economy; worker cooperative development; community economic democracy; Zone 5 New England cooperative research with Midwest applicability; academic + practitioner straddler
recruiting_angle: Her dual identity — academic researcher and practitioner network builder — means she can produce research-rigorous content in a practitioner voice; that straddler profile is the ideal author profile for Domain A

name: Sena Fitts
institution_organization: New Economy Coalition / Boston Impact Initiative
background: Community wealth building; local economy organizing; solidarity economy coordination; network building for economic alternatives; has written accessible guides on solidarity economy
recruiting_angle: NEC's work on community wealth building is the most practically-oriented US organization working on Domain A's core questions; Fitts's network access means the document would reach exactly the practitioners who need it
